type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: normalize_image_bytes() helper + get_image_mimetype() method + image_to_base64() validator
code: def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where encoded_image
    is guaranteed to be base64-encoded and decoded_for_guessing is the raw bytes
    suitable for MIME type detection.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except binascii.Error:
        decoded_for_guessing = image
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)

    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image: bytes) -> str | None:
        """Guess the mimetype of the image from its decoded bytes."""
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_image)
        return guess.mime if guess else None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)

        if not self.image_mimetype:
            self.image_mimetype = self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self
note: Merged: module-level normalize_image_bytes() handles base64 detection/encoding, ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype() handles mimetype guessing, image_to_base64() validator calls both. Need to add 'import binascii' too.

code: import base64
import binascii
...

def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where encoded_image
    is guaranteed to be base64-encoded and decoded_for_guessing is the raw bytes
    suitable for MIME type detection.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except binascii.Error:
        decoded_for_guessing = image
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)

    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    ...

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the mimetype of the image.

        If the mimetype has not been set yet, guess it from the given decoded
        bytes (or self.image if not provided) using the filetype library and
        cache the result for future accesses.
        """
        guess_source = decoded_image if decoded_image is not None else self.image
        if not self.image_mimetype and guess_source:
            guess = filetype.guess(guess_source)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self
note: Reconciling: normalize_image_bytes MUST return a tuple (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) per my task spec -- that's the point of the helper (guessing must happen on decoded/raw bytes, not the base64-encoded ones, otherwise filetype.guess breaks on the base64-encoded case and on already-encoded input). I extended your get_image_mimetype to accept an optional decoded_image param so it still works standalone (falls back to self.image) but the validator passes the correct decoded bytes explicitly. Also need 'import binascii' added at top. Let me know if this works for you.

import base64
import binascii
...

def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where encoded_image
    is guaranteed to be base64-encoded and decoded_for_guessing is the raw bytes
    suitable for MIME type detection.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except binascii.Error:
        decoded_for_guessing = image
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)

    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    ...

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the mimetype of the image.

        If the mimetype has not been set yet, guess it from the given decoded
        bytes (or self.image if not provided) using the filetype library and
        cache the result for future accesses.
        """
        guess_source = decoded_image if decoded_image is not None else self.image
        if not self.image_mimetype and guess_source:
            guess = filetype.guess(guess_source)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self
fields.note: Reconciling: normalize_image_bytes MUST return a tuple (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) per my task spec -- that's the point of the helper (guessing must happen on decoded/raw bytes, not the base64-encoded ones, otherwise filetype.guess breaks on the base64-encoded case and on already-encoded input). I extended your get_image_mimetype to accept an optional decoded_image param so it still works standalone (falls back to self.image) but the validator passes the correct decoded bytes explicitly. Also need 'import binascii' added at top. Let me know if this works for you.

region: get_image_mimetype() method + image_to_base64() validator (keeping normalize_image_bytes() as-is)
code:     def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the mimetype of the image.

        If the mimetype has not been set yet, guess it from the image bytes
        using the filetype library and cache the result for future accesses.
        """
        if not self.image_mimetype and self.image:
            decoded = base64.b64decode(self.image)
            guess = filetype.guess(decoded)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we wont load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, _ = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype()

        return self
